REMOVED FROM MASTER COURT CALENDAR SET FOR DEC-13-2021 - CASE SETTLED. ASSIGNED TO DEPT. 610 ON JAN-25-2022 AT 10:30 AM FOR DISMISSAL PER CRC 3.1385.
REPLY TO PLAINTIFF CHASTITY HAYES OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER (TRANSACTION ID # 200020833) FILED BY DEFENDANT HOSELEY CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302 DEFENDANT HOSELEY CORPORATION DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT APPEAR TO DISPUTE THAT HER SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION - FEHA HARASSMENT AND FEHA HOSTILE WORK ENVIRONMENT - ARE ENTIRELY DUPLICATIVE AS PLED, SO THAT DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. HOSELEY'S OTHER DEMURRERS ARE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF'S SIXTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION - WRONGFUL AND TORTIOUS DISCHARGE, AND WRONGFUL DISCHARGE IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY - HAVE DIFFERENT LEGAL BASES. (SEE, E.G., ROJO V. KLIGER (1990) 52 CAL.3D 65, 73.) AND "THE COMPLAINT AS A WHOLE CONTAIN[S] SUFFICIENT FACTS TO APPRISE THE DEFENDANT OF THE BASIS UPON WHICH THE PLAINTIFF IS SEEKING RELIEF." (PERKINS V. SUP. CT. (1981) 117 CAL.APP.3D 1, 6.) WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM.

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-11-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances by ZOOM Video-conferencing:
Attorney, Joshua S. Falakassa (Email: josh@falakassalaw.com) of the law offices of Falakassa Law, P.C. (Tel: 818.456.6168) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Jessica M. Peterson (Email: jmpeterson@grsm.com) of the law offices of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (Tel: 415.986.5900) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides, on record as reflected in the notes of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT HOSELEY CORPORATION DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part. Plaintiff does not appear to dispute that her second and third causes of action - FEHA harassment and FEHA hostile work environment - are entirely duplicative as pled, so that demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Hoseley's other demurrers are overruled. Plaintiff's sixth and seventh causes of action - wrongful and tortious discharge, and wrongful discharge in violation of public policy - have different legal bases. (See, e.g., Rojo v. Kliger (1990) 52 Cal.3d 65, 73.) And "the complaint as a whole contain[s] sufficient facts to apprise the defendant of the basis upon which the plaintiff is seeking relief." (Perkins v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 1, 6.)

Written order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com.
